seo_title: How COBALT Democratizes Robot Learning via Cloud-Based Teleoperation…
meta_description: COBALT enables scalable robot learning at scale using smartphones, reducing teleoperation cost and improving data quality.
body_html:
COBALT enables scalable robot learning at scale using smartphones, reducing teleoperation cost and improving data quality.

Concurrent teleoperation by multiple users on a single GPU Supports up to 8 concurrent users per GPU with sub-100 ms end-to-end latency Operators can connect from nearly anywhere on Earth using commonly available devices

Improved Data Quality COBALT logs a suite of real-time metrics to automatically filter suboptimal demonstrations.

Faster Data Collection Phone-based teleoperation performs comparably to or better than specialized hardware, enabling faster data collection.

Scalability and Performance COBALT supports 256 simulated clients across 8 GPUs, underscoring the system's ability to scale across hardware and within individual servers.

Phone-based teleoperation performs comparably to or better than specialized hardware in terms of data collection quality. The structured user training curriculum significantly improves data collection quality.

source.title: COBALT: Crowdsourcing Robot Learning via Cloud-Based Teleoperation with Smartphones
source.summary: Researchers have developed COBALT, a platform that enables crowdsourced robot learning via cloud-based teleoperation using smartphones. The system allows multiple users to control robots in real-time and collect high-quality demonstration data, which can be used to train imitation learning algorithms. By leveraging vectorized environments and scalable infrastructure, COBALT reduces the cost of teleoperation and can support dozens of concurrent users with low latency. A large-scale pilot dataset was also collected using smartphones across nine countries, demonstrating the platform's potential for democratizing robot learning at scale.
